# Let's load the quranic_verses.json file.
import json, os, sys, re

# Try to find the file path in /mnt/data
for fname in os.listdir('/mnt/data'):
    print(fname)

qfile = '/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json'
hfile = '/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json'

with open(qfile, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    q = json.load(f)

# Inspect structure
type(q), len(q)

# show sample items
for i in range(0,5):
    print(i, list(q[i].keys()))
    print(q[i].get('ayah_text')[:200])
    print()

response_context = """القرآن الكريم لا يربط التنازع أو النزاع بالفشل بشكل مباشر، بل يربطه بالنية والهدف والسبب. ففي بعض الأحيان، يُعتبر التنازع جزءًا من الطريق لتحقيق النجاح والتمكين، بينما في أحيان أخرى، يُعتبر نتيجة للفشل أو الخطيئة. إليك توضيحًا لهذه الفكرة مع استشهاد من القرآن الكريم والأحاديث النبوية:
### 1. **التفاوت بين النزاع المبرر والنزاع غير المبرر** 
القرآن يفرق بين النزاع الذي يتم به مصلحة عظيمة (كالقتال في سبيل الله) والنزاع الذي يُستغل للفساد أو الخطيئة. 
- **القتال في سبيل الله**: 
 في سورة البقرة (2:217)، يُذكر قصة معركة بدر، حيث أخبر الله تعالى أن المؤمنين كانوا قلة، لكنه ساعدهم بفضل إيمانهم ونية حسنة: 
 > **"وَكَانَتْ أُمَّتُكُمْ وَاحِدَةً فَأَشْرَكْتُمْ فَأَفْرَقْنَا بَيْنَكُمْ وَلَنْ تَرْجُو لِلْعَذَابِ عَذَابًا وَاللَّهُ قَدْ أَحَاطَ بِكُمْ وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِب"
"""
start = 614
end = 837
print(len(response_context))
print("Extracted:", repr(response_context[start:end]))

# Build normalization functions and search.

import re
import unicodedata
from difflib import SequenceMatcher

# Arabic diacritics unicode range: \u0610-\u061A, \u064B-\u065F, \u06D6-\u06ED
arabic_diacritics_re = re.compile("""[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u06D6-\u06ED\u0670]""")

def normalize_ar(text):
    # remove tashkeel (diacritics), tatweel, punctuation, unify hamza forms
    text = text or ""
    # remove diacritics
    text = arabic_diacritics_re.sub("", text)
    # remove tatweel
    text = text.replace('\u0640', '')
    # normalize hamza forms to bare hamza ??? unify to ا? common normalization: أإآ to ا, ئ to ي? but cautious
    # Replace various hamza forms:
    text = text.replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا')
    # normalize alef maksura to ي
    text = text.replace('ى','ي')
    # replace taa marbuta to ه? or to ت? Keep as is maybe convert ة to ه? Common normalization: ة->ه or to ت? For search, maybe replace with ه? But leave.
    text = text.replace('ؤ','و').replace('ئ','ي')
    # remove non-letters and punctuation
    text = re.sub(r'[^ء-ي0-9\s]', ' ', text)  # punctuation removed, keep Arabic letters and digits and spaces
    # collapse spaces
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    # lower() not needed for arabic
    return text

def tokenize_ar(text):
    return normalize_ar(text).split()

def lcs_length(a,b):
    # longest common substring (continuous) length using dynamic programming
    A = a
    B = b
    n = len(A); m = len(B)
    dp = [[0]*(m+1) for _ in range(n+1)]
    longest = 0
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(m):
            if A[i] == B[j]:
                dp[i+1][j+1] = dp[i][j] + 1
                if dp[i+1][j+1] > longest:
                    longest = dp[i+1][j+1]
    return longest

def similarity_metrics(s1, s2):
    # normalized s1,s2
    n1 = normalize_ar(s1)
    n2 = normalize_ar(s2)
    if len(n1)==0 or len(n2)==0:
        return {'seq_ratio': 0.0, 'lcs':0.0, 'token_f1':0.0}
    # sequence matcher ratio
    seq_ratio = SequenceMatcher(None, n1, n2).ratio()
    # LCS ratio normalized by max length
    lcs = lcs_length(n1, n2) / max(len(n1), len(n2))
    # token F1
    t1 = tokenize_ar(s1)
    t2 = tokenize_ar(s2)
    if len(t1)==0 or len(t2)==0:
        token_f1 = 0.0
    else:
        set1 = t1[:]  # keep duplicates? use counters
        from collections import Counter
        c1 = Counter(t1)
        c2 = Counter(t2)
        # compute overlap count
        overlap = sum(min(c1[t], c2.get(t,0)) for t in c1)
        precision = overlap / sum(c2.values()) if sum(c2.values())>0 else 0.0
        recall = overlap / sum(c1.values()) if sum(c1.values())>0 else 0.0
        if precision+recall==0:
            token_f1 = 0.0
        else:
            token_f1 = 2*precision*recall/(precision+recall)
    return {'seq_ratio':seq_ratio, 'lcs':lcs, 'token_f1':token_f1, 'avg': (seq_ratio + lcs + token_f1)/3.0}

# Let's test normalization on the provided span and known ayah sample
provided_span = "وَكَانَتْ أُمَّتُكُمْ وَاحِدَةً فَأَشْرَكْتُمْ فَأَفْرَقْنَا بَيْنَكُمْ وَلَنْ تَرْجُو لِلْعَذَابِ عَذَابًا وَاللَّهُ قَدْ أَحَاطَ بِكُمْ وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِب"
print(normalize_ar(provided_span))

# search for candidates containing "يتق الله يجعل" normalized
target_phrases = ["من يتق الله يجعل له مخرجا","يتق الله يجعل له مخرجا","يرزقه من حيث لا يحتسب","من حيث لا يحتسب","ولن ترجو للعذاب عذابا","قد احاط بكم","واحدة فاشركتم","وكانت امتكم واحدة"]

candidates = []
for i, item in enumerate(q):
    text = item.get('ayah_text','')
    norm = normalize_ar(text)
    for ph in target_phrases:
        if ph in norm:
            candidates.append((i, item['surah_name'], item['ayah_id'], text, ph))
            break

len(candidates), candidates[:10]

# search for phrase `كانت امتك` normalized
ph = "كانت امتكم واحدة"
matches = []
for i,item in enumerate(q):
    norm = normalize_ar(item.get('ayah_text',''))
    if ph in norm:
        matches.append((i, item['surah_name'], item['ayah_id'], item['ayah_text']))
matches[:20], len(matches)

# search for word 'فأشركتم' (normalize)
ph="فاشركتم"
matches=[]
for i,item in enumerate(q):
    if ph in normalize_ar(item.get('ayah_text','')):
        matches.append((i,item['surah_name'],item['ayah_id'],item['ayah_text']))
len(matches), matches[:20]

cand1 = q[5218]['ayah_text']
cand2 = q[5219]['ayah_text']
candidate_combined = cand1 + " " + cand2
full_sub = response_context[start:end]  # extracted earlier
provided_span = "وَكَانَتْ أُمَّتُكُمْ وَاحِدَةً فَأَشْرَكْتُمْ فَأَفْرَقْنَا بَيْنَكُمْ وَلَنْ تَرْجُو لِلْعَذَابِ عَذَابًا وَاللَّهُ قَدْ أَحَاطَ بِكُمْ وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِب"
print("Candidate combined:", candidate_combined)
print("Full substring:", full_sub)
print("Provided span:", provided_span)
print("Normalized cand:", normalize_ar(candidate_combined))
print("Normalized full:", normalize_ar(full_sub))
print("Metrics (candidate vs full):", similarity_metrics(candidate_combined, full_sub))

# find where "من يتق الله يجعل" occurs in full_sub normalized
norm_full = normalize_ar(full_sub)
norm_full.find("من يتق الله يجعل")

sub_from_m = full_sub[ full_sub.find("وَمَنْ") if full_sub.find("وَمَنْ")!=-1 else full_sub.find("مَنْ") : ]
# but full_sub has diacritics; use direct 'وَمَنْ' search
print("Sub from 'وَمَنْ':", repr(sub_from_m))
print("Normalized sub:", normalize_ar(sub_from_m))
print("Similarity candidate vs sub_from_m:", similarity_metrics(candidate_combined, sub_from_m))

# compute similarity for single verses
import math
best_single = []
for i,item in enumerate(q):
    sim = similarity_metrics(item.get('ayah_text',''), full_sub)['avg']
    best_single.append((sim, i, item['surah_name'], item['ayah_id'], item['ayah_text']))

best_single_sorted = sorted(best_single, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
best_single_sorted[:10]

# compute best combos of consecutive ayat (i and i+1)
best_combo = []
for i in range(len(q)-1):
    cand = q[i]['ayah_text'] + " " + q[i+1]['ayah_text']
    sim = similarity_metrics(cand, full_sub)['avg']
    best_combo.append((sim, i, q[i]['surah_name'], q[i]['ayah_id'], q[i+1]['ayah_id'], cand))

best_combo_sorted = sorted(best_combo, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
best_combo_sorted[:15]

with open(hfile, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    had = json.load(f)

len(had), type(had[0])

# find Surah Baqara ayah 217
baqara_217 = [item for item in q if item['surah_name']=='البقرة' and item['ayah_id']==217]
baqara_217

# find Baqara 213
baqara_213 = [item for item in q if item['surah_name']=='البقرة' and item['ayah_id']==213]
baqara_213

content.text: {"correction":"فَإِذَا بَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ فَارِقُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ وَأَشْهِدُوا ذَوَيْ عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ وَأَقِيمُوا الشَّهَادَةَ لِلَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ يُوعَظُ بِهِ مَنْ كَانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ ۚ وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ ۚ وَمَنْ يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بَالِغُ أَمْرِهِ ۚ قَدْ جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدْرًا"}
